engine_note: Ran under the 3-pro-preview default that was in tools/coptic/run_coptic_ocr.py at the time. Outputs are clean Coptic (100% Coptic-letter coverage on every fragment). The default model has since been corrected to gemini-3.1-pro-preview; rerun under 3.1-pro is not urgent and can happen opportunistically.
instructions: Transcribe Coptic exactly before any normalization.
Preserve line breaks and page boundaries when visible.
Mark uncertain or damaged letters explicitly instead of guessing.
Do not silently harmonize spelling or punctuation.
Emit plaintext plus a short uncertainty note if the witness is hard to read.
notes: Fragmentary XII.2 overlap witness for damaged or uncertain stretches of I.3.

fragments.approximate_i3_alignment: NHC I.3 pp. 30-31 area — Book of the Living / early Return to Unity
fragments.alignment_basis: Attridge NHS 22-23 scholarly consensus on XII.2 extents (Zone 2 fact-level citation). Exact per-line alignment to I.3 refined during drafting.

fragments.approximate_i3_alignment: NHC I.3 pp. 31-32 area — Return to Unity
NHC I.3 pp. 34-36 area — Return to Unity / Parable of the Jars
NHC I.3 pp. 35-37 area — Parable of the Jars / Coming into Being
Multi-location fragment bucket; per-line assignment to I.3 segments deferred to drafting.
fragments.alignment_basis: Requires per-line scholarly alignment against the full NHC XII plate sequence during the drafting phase.

drafting_use: At drafting time, for any Gospel of Truth segment listed under coverage_segments above, the translator prompt should include the corresponding fragment's Coptic text as an overlay/cross-check witness to NHC I.3. Where XII.2 and I.3 disagree, the disagreement gets an explicit per-verse footnote rather than silent harmonization.
